payable.name: Sales Negotiation Mastery
coreBooks: Never Split the Difference — Chris Voss
Getting to Yes — Fisher & Ury
payable.microPractice: Practice one negotiation tactic in today's conversation — anchor first, then listen.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: use one named tactic (mirroring, labeling, calibrated questions) in a real situation.

chapterMap.1: Never Split the Difference: Ch.1–2 — Active listening + Tactical empathy
chapterMap.2: Never Split the Difference: Ch.3–4 — Mirroring + Labeling
chapterMap.3: Never Split the Difference: Ch.5–6 — Mastering No + Bending Reality
chapterMap.4: Getting to Yes: Part 1 — Don't bargain over positions
chapterMap.5: Getting to Yes: Part 2 — The method: focus on interests
chapterMap.6: Never Split the Difference: Ch.7–8 — Calibrated questions + Guarantee execution
chapterMap.7: Getting to Yes: Part 3 — Alternatives + BATNA development
chapterMap.8: Never Split the Difference: Ch.9–10 — Bargaining hard + Finding the Black Swan
chapterMap.9: Full review: write your personal negotiation playbook from both books

payable.name: Human Nature
coreBooks: The Laws of Human Nature — Robert Greene
Thinking, Fast and Slow — Daniel Kahneman
payable.microPractice: Observe one person today and identify one cognitive bias they display.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: correctly predict one person's behavior based on a law of human nature.

chapterMap.1: Laws of Human Nature: Ch.1 — Master your emotional self
chapterMap.2: Thinking Fast and Slow: Part 1 — System 1 vs System 2
chapterMap.3: Laws of Human Nature: Ch.2 — Transform self-love into empathy
chapterMap.4: Thinking Fast and Slow: Part 2 — Heuristics and biases
chapterMap.5: Laws of Human Nature: Ch.3 — See through people's masks
chapterMap.6: Thinking Fast and Slow: Part 3 — Overconfidence
chapterMap.7: Laws of Human Nature: Ch.4 — Determine the strength of people's character
chapterMap.8: Thinking Fast and Slow: Part 4 — Choices under uncertainty
chapterMap.9: Full synthesis: map 5 biases you personally have to laws of human nature

payable.name: Emotional Intelligence
coreBooks: Emotional Intelligence 2.0 — Bradberry & Greaves
The Emotional Brain — Joseph LeDoux
payable.microPractice: Name three emotions you felt today and what triggered each.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: correctly identify your emotional state before reacting in 5 situations.

chapterMap.1: EQ 2.0: Introduction — What EQ is and why IQ isn't enough
chapterMap.9: Full review: take the EQ self-assessment and write your improvement plan

payable.name: Critical Thinking
coreBooks: Thinking in Systems — Donella Meadows
The Art of Thinking Clearly — Rolf Dobelli
payable.microPractice: Take one assumption you hold and argue the opposite position for 3 minutes.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: spot 5 thinking errors in the wild and name them precisely.

chapterMap.1: Thinking in Systems: Part 1 — System structure: stocks and flows
chapterMap.2: Art of Thinking Clearly: Biases 1–10 (Survivorship bias, Swimmer's body illusion...)
chapterMap.3: Thinking in Systems: Part 2 — System traps and opportunities
chapterMap.4: Art of Thinking Clearly: Biases 11–20 (Clustering illusion, Social proof...)
chapterMap.5: Thinking in Systems: Part 3 — Creating change in systems
chapterMap.8: Art of Thinking Clearly: Biases 51–99 (deep dive on 5 most relevant to you)
chapterMap.9: Full synthesis: map 3 system traps you're currently in and your escape plan

payable.name: Effective Communication
coreBooks: Crucial Conversations — Patterson, Grenny et al.
Simply Said — Jay Sullivan
payable.microPractice: Before your next message, write one sentence on what you want the reader to DO after reading it.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: have one crucial conversation you've been avoiding.

chapterMap.1: Crucial Conversations: Ch.1–2 — What makes a conversation crucial + Mastering our stories
chapterMap.2: Simply Said: Part 1 — Communicating clearly: focus on the audience
chapterMap.3: Crucial Conversations: Ch.3–4 — Start with heart + Learn to look
chapterMap.4: Simply Said: Part 2 — Verbal communication (presentations, conversations)
chapterMap.5: Crucial Conversations: Ch.5–6 — Make it safe + Master your story
chapterMap.6: Simply Said: Part 3 — Written communication (email, reports)
chapterMap.7: Crucial Conversations: Ch.7–8 — STATE your path + Explore others' paths
chapterMap.8: Crucial Conversations: Ch.9–10 — Move to action + Putting it all together
chapterMap.9: Full review: write your communication manifesto — how you'll show up in every conversation

payable.name: Time Management
coreBooks: Deep Work — Cal Newport
The One Thing — Gary Keller
payable.microPractice: Write your top 3 outcomes for tomorrow before you sleep. Nothing else counts.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: complete 5 deep work blocks of 90+ minutes each with zero interruptions.

chapterMap.1: Deep Work: Part 1 — The idea: deep work is valuable, rare, and meaningful
chapterMap.2: The One Thing: Part 1 — The lies: multitasking, willpower, and a balanced life
chapterMap.3: Deep Work: Part 2 — The rules: work deeply (schedule + rituals)
chapterMap.4: The One Thing: Part 2 — The truth: the focusing question
chapterMap.5: Deep Work: Part 2 — The rules: embrace boredom + quit social media
chapterMap.6: The One Thing: Part 3 — Extraordinary results: live with purpose + priority
chapterMap.7: Deep Work: Part 2 — The rules: drain the shallows (fixed schedule productivity)
chapterMap.8: Synthesis: design your ideal weekly schedule using both frameworks
chapterMap.9: Full review: run your new schedule for 7 days. Write what changed.

payable.name: Problem Solving
coreBooks: How to Solve It — George Pólya
Algorithms to Live By — Brian Christian
payable.microPractice: Pick one open problem you have. Write the real root cause in one sentence — not the symptom.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: solve 3 real problems using Pólya's 4-step method. Document each.

chapterMap.1: How to Solve It: Part 1 — In the classroom: understanding the problem
chapterMap.2: Algorithms to Live By: Ch.1–2 — Optimal stopping + Explore/exploit
chapterMap.3: How to Solve It: Part 2 — How to solve it (the heuristics)
chapterMap.4: Algorithms to Live By: Ch.3–4 — Sorting + Caching
chapterMap.5: How to Solve It: Part 3 — Short dictionary of heuristics (A–H)
chapterMap.6: Algorithms to Live By: Ch.5–7 — Scheduling + Bayes + Overfitting
chapterMap.7: How to Solve It: Part 3 — Short dictionary of heuristics (I–Z)
chapterMap.8: Algorithms to Live By: Ch.8–11 — Relaxation + Randomness + Networking + Game theory
chapterMap.9: Full synthesis: write your personal problem-solving framework combining both approaches

payable.name: Creativity and Innovation
coreBooks: Steal Like an Artist — Austin Kleon
A Technique for Producing Ideas — James Webb Young
payable.microPractice: Combine two unrelated concepts and write three ways they could intersect in your work.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: generate one genuinely novel idea that you're excited enough to prototype.

chapterMap.1: Steal Like an Artist: Ch.1–3 — Steal, don't wait to know who you are, write what you love
chapterMap.2: A Technique for Producing Ideas: Stage 1 — Gathering raw material
chapterMap.3: Steal Like an Artist: Ch.4–6 — Use your hands, side projects, secret work
chapterMap.4: A Technique for Producing Ideas: Stage 2 — Mental digestion
chapterMap.5: Steal Like an Artist: Ch.7–9 — Geography, be boring, be kind
chapterMap.6: A Technique for Producing Ideas: Stage 3–5 — The Eureka moment + Shaping the idea
chapterMap.7: Full Steal Like an Artist re-read with your current project in mind
chapterMap.8: Apply the 5-stage technique to one real problem you're solving right now
chapterMap.9: Build and share one thing you've been afraid to make. Publish it.

payable.name: Mental Resilience
coreBooks: Can't Hurt Me — David Goggins
The Obstacle Is the Way — Ryan Holiday
payable.microPractice: Deliberately do one uncomfortable thing today — cold exposure, a hard conversation, or a task you've been avoiding.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: do one thing every day that you wanted to avoid. Log each one.

chapterMap.1: Can't Hurt Me: Ch.1–2 — Darkness to light + Taking souls
chapterMap.2: The Obstacle Is the Way: Part 1 — Perception: seeing clearly
chapterMap.3: Can't Hurt Me: Ch.3–4 — The impossible task + Taking inventory
chapterMap.4: The Obstacle Is the Way: Part 2 — Action: moving forward
chapterMap.5: Can't Hurt Me: Ch.5–6 — Armored mind + Cookie jar
chapterMap.6: The Obstacle Is the Way: Part 3 — Will: preparing for adversity
chapterMap.7: Can't Hurt Me: Ch.7–8 — The 40% rule + Talent not required
chapterMap.8: Can't Hurt Me: Ch.9–10 — Uncommon amongst uncommon + The empowerment of failure
chapterMap.9: Full synthesis: write your personal resilience manifesto. What breaks you. What builds you.

payable.name: Money Management
coreBooks: The Psychology of Money — Morgan Housel
I Will Teach You to Be Rich — Ramit Sethi
payable.microPractice: Review last week's spending in 5 minutes. Identify one thing to cut and one to increase.
payable.weeklyGoal: By end of week: automate one financial process (savings, bill, investment).

chapterMap.1: Psychology of Money: Ch.1–3 — No one's crazy + Luck and risk + Never enough
chapterMap.2: I Will Teach You to Be Rich: Ch.1–2 — Credit cards + Banks
chapterMap.3: Psychology of Money: Ch.4–6 — Confounding compounding + Getting wealthy vs staying wealthy
chapterMap.4: I Will Teach You to Be Rich: Ch.3–4 — Investing + Conscious spending
chapterMap.5: Psychology of Money: Ch.7–9 — Freedom + Man in the car paradox + Wealth is what you don't see
chapterMap.6: I Will Teach You to Be Rich: Ch.5–6 — Saving + The I Will Teach You to Be Rich system
chapterMap.7: Psychology of Money: Ch.10–13 — Save money + Reasonable over rational + Surprise
chapterMap.8: Psychology of Money: Ch.14–20 — Room for error to Confessions
chapterMap.9: Full action: set up your automated financial system. Document it. Run it.
